pubmed_links: [Wong et al. (2017) The PHF21B gene is associated with major depression and modulates the stress response](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27777418/); [Chin et al. (2022) The epigenetic reader PHF21B modulates murine social memory and synaptic plasticity-related genes](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35866480/); [Basu et al. (2020) Phf21b imprints the spatiotemporal epigenetic switch essential for neural stem cell differentiation](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32820037/)
summary: PHF21B is an epigenetic reader whose rare variants are associated with major depressive disorder; mouse models show that PHF21B depletion impairs social memory and reduces synaptic plasticity-related gene expression in the hippocampus. (emerging evidence)
